article_title: Ketamine as Antidepressant? Current State and Future Perspectives

aggregated_relations.subject: low synaptic levels of monoamines
evidence_sentences: In the original theoretical form, low synaptic levels of monoamines (especially noradrenaline and serotonin) were assumed to directly trigger MDD [9].

aggregated_relations.subject: monoaminergic drugs
evidence_sentences: The monoamine view was developed, like so many seminal discoveries in psychiatry, after accidental findings that monoaminergic drugs also alleviate depressive symptoms [11].

aggregated_relations.subject: isoniazid
evidence_sentences: For instance, the monoamine oxidase inhibitor isoniazid, originally employed in tuberculosis treatment, was found to improve mood [12].

aggregated_relations.subject: reserpine
evidence_sentences: In keeping with the monoamine hypothesis, the anti-hypertensive drug reserpine can induce depression because it antagonizes monoamines (but see [14]).

aggregated_relations.subject: reserpine
evidence_sentences: In keeping with the monoamine hypothesis, the anti-hypertensive drug reserpine can induce depression because it antagonizes monoamines (but see [14]).

aggregated_relations.subject: deficient synaptic levels
evidence_sentences: Even though deficient synaptic levels cause postsynaptic receptor upregulation, monoaminergic neurotransmission could be blunted because of receptor abnormalities [15].

aggregated_relations.subject: antidepressant treatment
evidence_sentences: Indeed, antidepressant treatment has been shown to lead to postsynaptic receptor downregulation, temporally consistent with therapeutic delay (for a review, see [16]).

aggregated_relations.subject: MAOIs
evidence_sentences: MAOIs inhibit enzyme activity and thereby non-specifically increase synaptic monoamine availability, which can cause severe side effects if used incautiously.

aggregated_relations.subject: Consumption of foodstuff high in tyramine or tryptophan
evidence_sentences: Consumption of foodstuff high in tyramine or tryptophan (e.g. red wine or beer) has the potential to trigger hypertensive crises or serotonin syndrome, reflecting the sudden surge in transmitter availability and impaired synaptic clearance [29].

aggregated_relations.subject: synaptic serotonin
evidence_sentences: The surge in synaptic serotonin availability leads to somatodendritic 5-HT1Aautoreceptor desensitization in Raphé cells, disinhibiting further cellular serotonin release.

aggregated_relations.subject: 5-HT1A autoreceptor desensitization
evidence_sentences: The surge in synaptic serotonin availability leads to somatodendritic 5-HT1Aautoreceptor desensitization in Raphé cells, disinhibiting further cellular serotonin release.

aggregated_relations.subject: extracellular serotonin
evidence_sentences: Increased extracellular serotonin levels then induce adaptive changes such as postsynaptic downregulation of some receptor types (especially 5-HT1A and 5-HT2A), which are hypothesized to eventually mediate mood improvements [36].

aggregated_relations.subject: SNRIs
evidence_sentences: SNRIs are dual reuptake inhibitors like TCAs and MAOIs (i.e. they block serotonin and noradrenaline transporter proteins), but exhibit higher safety and tolerability because of chemical specificity [33].

chunks_extracted.section: THE GLUTAMATE SYSTEM: A NEW TARGET

aggregated_relations.subject: Glutamic acid decarboxylase
evidence_sentences: Glutamic acid decarboxylase transforms glutamate into gamma-Aminobutyric acid (GABA), while glutamine synthetase degrades glutamate to glutamine.

aggregated_relations.subject: glutamine synthetase
evidence_sentences: Glutamic acid decarboxylase transforms glutamate into gamma-Aminobutyric acid (GABA), while glutamine synthetase degrades glutamate to glutamine.

aggregated_relations.subject: glutamate transporter proteins
evidence_sentences: The effects of synaptic glutamate are terminated by glutamate transporter proteins, triggering glial reuptake and subsequent metabolization.

aggregated_relations.subject: glutamate
evidence_sentences: Thus, extracellular glutamate homeostasis is tightly regulated as too little glutamate impairs synaptic plasticity while too much causes oxidative damage and excitotoxicity [48].

aggregated_relations.subject: glutamate transporter 1 (GT1) inhibition
evidence_sentences: Intriguingly, glutamate transporter 1 (GT1) inhibition in prefrontal areas induces anhedonia in a rat model of depression

Intriguingly, glutamate transporter 1 (GT1) inhibition in prefrontal areas induces anhedonia in a rat model of depression [60], paralleling reduced GT1 activity in mediotemporal/frontal areas (but also locus coeruleus, see [61]) during MDD in humans [62-64].

aggregated_relations.subject: antidepressants
evidence_sentences: Psychopharmacological research has shown extensively that some functional antidepressants downregulate glutamatergic neurotransmission, including fluoxetine, buproprion, urcumin, lamotrigine, riluzole, imipramine and phenelzine

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: Beside NMDAR receptor antagonism, it seems ketamine blocks muscarinic acetylcholine receptors, interacts with CN1 channels and possesses some affinity for both σ receptors [82], which could explain why it modulates nerve growth factor (NGF) production and synaptic remodeling [86].

aggregated_relations.subject: ketamine
evidence_sentences: Indeed, a study by Duman and Li [89] showed a single injection of ketamine increased prefrontal synaptogenesis and reversed stress-induced atrophy.

evidence_sentences: Furthermore, ketamine possesses a weak affinity for mµ and K opioid receptors [91], but a high affinity for D2 and 5-HT2A receptors, where it commonly acts as an agonist [92].

aggregated_relations.subject: oral consumption
evidence_sentences: Oral consumption leads to higher concentrations of norketamine, a metabolite predominantly associated with sedative effects, which represents a clear drawback of this route of administration [82].

aggregated_relations.subject: high doses
evidence_sentences: Memantine could be such a substance, because high doses cause psychedelic experiences similar to ketamine, while at the same time being devoid of antidepressant impact [175,176].
